title: festuca littoralis
intro: Festuca littoralis, also known as salt meadow-grass or littoral fescue, is a species of grass that is native to salt marshes and coastal areas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Festuca littoralis include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and is often used topically to treat skin conditions and promote tissue repair.
